records.m_pText: Ancheim, Land of Sand and Time
I never expected to find such$na clockwork wonder in the$nmiddle of a desert.
Yeah, it’s great, but this heat!$nUgh… I feel gross.
The heat was always allayed by a$nsteady breeze, but now…
The wind is stopped,$nthe air stagnant.
What about the water?$nIs that contraption drawing$nup drinking water?
I believe so. I recall hearing$nit was quite costly,$nlast I was here.
Well, it is an impressive$nmachine… Wait, you mean$nthe water!?
Indeed. I was surprised to learn$nwater came free in Caldisla.
I had always known it as$nsomething to be bought, so you$ncan imagine my delight.
More importantly, [PCF1],$nwhat of the women here?$nAre they beautiful?$nPassionate? Devoted?
Where would you suggest for a$ndate spot? I’ll need a list of$ngood restaurants if you ple—
Unacceptable.
[PCF1]’s Angst
You seemed on edge during that$nwhole speech.
You don’t have to bottle it all$nin. I’m no expert, but I’m$nhappy to listen.
I cannot deny that the people of$nAncheim are dissatisfied with$nmy work as vestal.
I’ve no intention of making$nexcuses.
But you didn’t run away, either.$nRight?
…The king asked me directly.
He said that terrible things$nwere happening in Caldisla, and$nasked that I investigate.
Yet now he paints an entirely$ndifferent picture. I cannot$nunderstand his need to lie.
Does he bear me some grudge?$nHave I done him some slight?
Do you regret listening to$nhim in the first place?
…No. I do not regret my time$nin Caldisla.
Seeing that chasm firsthand$nshowed me precisely what$nit is I must do.
And you met [PCM1]$nthere, too!
That’s—$nAiry, that’s hardly…
I’m glad you came and saw the$nchasm, too.
You’ve shown me a path forward.
…Blessed is the person who$nis needed by someone else.
Hmm? What’s this?$nWhat are we talking about?
N-nothing! It’s nothing.
Then why are you hiding it?$nCome on, don’t leave me out.$nI want to know!
Unacceptable.$nTo me, and to [PCM1].$nRight, [PCM1]?
Er, yes, ma’am.
What’s This Temple?
So, what is this temple like,$nexactly? I have no idea what$nto expect.
It is a holy place that has been$na center of worship for$nmillennia.
To each crystal is wed a vestal,$nwho spends her lifetime in$nsteady prayer.
Vestalings—girls chosen as$nsuccessors to the vestals—go$nto live in the temples.
They leave its premises only$nvery rarely.
And so, to a vestal, the temple$nis also a home.
A flower garden,$nwith no boys allowed.
Be quiet, [PCM2].
So you were inside the temple$never since you became a$nvestaling?
It was quite rare to leave,$nsave for a few public rites$neach year.
Does that mean you were all$nalone? Wasn’t that hard?
I was not alone, no. The mother$nvestal—my predecessor—raised$nme to serve in her place.
And there were the acolytes.$nThey would go into town and$npurchase all we needed.
But you left for this journey. I$nguess that’s proof it’s$nimportant.
No. Even so, I had never$nintended to leave the temple.
Never intended any of this…
Vestal and Acolytes
What was daily life here like$nfor you, [PCF1]?
Life in the temple?
The acolytes and I offered our$nprayers to the crystal every$nday without fail.
Oof, no days off?
The sole purpose for the vestal$nand acolytes’ existence is to see$nthose prayers go on unbroken.
I see…
Each day, we would awaken with$nthe sun and perform morning$nprayers.
Ack, I couldn’t drag myself out$nof bed that early.
That’s just about bedtime$nfor me.
Sure, because you’re out$nphilandering all night.
So if you wake up at dawn, when$ndo you go to sleep?
Our days ended with the sun’s$nsetting.
That all sounds very$ndisciplined. Harsh, even.
Perhaps. But I cherished it. Even$nnow, I long to return to that$nlife dearly…
Such Sweet Sorrow
“How about a nip of breakfast$nfor an old man?” Hah! He cooks$njust fine.
Quite. It was nothing especially$nfancy or out of the ordinary,$nbut it went down fine.
I thought it was really good,$nactually.
Likewise. I wonder why he would$nhave wanted you to cook for$nhim, [PCF2].
All alone, out here in the$nwoods? Maybe he missed the$ntaste of the big city.
Aww, when you put it that way, I$nfeel bad for saying no.
What sort of food do you like$nto cook, [PCF2]?
Hmmm, I haven’t cooked enough$nto have a specialty or anything,$nbut if I had to pick something…
I make a mean jam and butter$nomelet.
…Beg your pardon?
Or whipped chestnut and sweet$npotato toast.
Uhh…
Or have you ever tried$nmarshmallow and condensed$nmilk risotto?
Er, no, but it sounds sweet…$nPainfully so.
Yup! Mother and Father were$nleft speechless by its amazing$ndeliciousness.
I’d be happy to whip some up for$nyou next time.
R-right…$nNext time…
The Cogs of Ancheim
Yaaaaaawn…
Just woke up and already that$ntired, [PCM1]?
It’s this city. You notice how$nthere are always gears turning$nsomeplace close?
Sure, the sound is deafening, at$ntimes. Little wonder this place$nis known for its grand clock.
The noise is bad, but the$nvibration is worse. I can’t$nget to sleep with that hum.
I’m surprised you’re so$nhigh-strung.
Surprised…?
I’m jealous of your ability$nto pass out anytime,$nanywhere, [PCF2].
Do you need a bit more rest,$n[PCM1]?
Oh, no. I’ll be fine.$nLet’s get going.
[PCM2]’s Reconnaissance
Just a moment, [PCM1].
Huh? What is it?
Hmm?$nSomething wrong?
Nothing at all.$nThis is a matter between$nmen, my dear.
Uh huh…
Now, where was I?$nAh, [PCM1]!
Last night, I chanced to awaken$nand notice you and [PCF1]$nwere absent from your beds.
What’s more, you took your time$ncoming back. Anything you’d care$nto confess, old chap?
Just what trouble does a stick-$nin-the-mud like you get up to?
Wha— That’s none of your$nbusiness!
Oh, come on, out with it! All of$nit, every last sordid, juicy,$nscandalous morsel! Out!
…I woke up and saw she wasn’t$nin her bed.
Yes? Yes!? Then what!?
I got out of bed to look for$nher.
Right, right. Sensible enough.$nGo on. Get to the good part!
I heard a sewing machine$nrunning, so I went to the$nsage’s work space and$nfound them talking.
What? Talk about a turn for the$nboring…
He listened to what was on her$nmind and offered her some$nadvice.
I promised myself I’d do a better$njob of helping her, too.
Hmph. Yes. Alright.$nSo, is that it, then?
That’s it. What did you expect?$nWe were only gone maybe fifteen$nminutes.
And besides, why were you up?
Hm? Me? Er, you know…
Anyway, I nodded off after a few$nminutes of watching [PCF2]$nsleep.
Yeah…
Alright, enough whispering.$nJust what is it, you two?
Ahem. Nothing at all, as I said!$nEh, [PCM1]? Ha ha ha…
The Minister’s Generosity
Ahh, leave it to the prime$nminister. He thought of$neverything!
Hm? What did he give us?$nIt’s…a piece of paper. Great.
Don’t be silly, my angel.$nThis is the bill of goods.$nThey’ll be sent to the$nship later.
First off, there’s the food.$nHe’s arranged for bread,$ncured meat, salted grassbeans…
Fruit celery, green turnips,$ntwo barrels of pickled pink$neggplant, Clocktower-brand$nfiraga sauce…
Then a barrel of Ancheim’s most$ntooth-rotting desserts. They$ndo like their sugar.
There’s at least two weeks’$nprovisions here.
Past food, there’s… Ah!$nBerthing permission for the$nEschalot! That’ll come in handy.
We can take on water, oil, even$nammunition. Truly, the man is a$nbureaucratic wizard.
Sounds like we’ll have a little$nparty waiting for us at the ship!
A party? We must make for$nFlorem as soon as humanly$npossible.
Well, as soon as possible is at$nleast half a day from now, given$nthe time to load all this.
Right. I think we’ve all earned at$nleast that much of a rest. Let’s$nenjoy it while we can.
Proof, Not Promises
The wind ought now return.
I’m sure the people of Ancheim$nwill be happy!
Yes, with the wind returned,$nthe kingdom can go back to$nits former, peaceful ways.
Let’s hurry back and report.$nI want to see that lousy$nking squirm!
I’m still mad at what he did to$nyou before, [PCF1].
Mark my words, he’s black as$ntar!
You agree with me, don’t you?$nYou have to!
Not all of us are so set on$nseeing the world in terms of$nblack and white, [PCF2].
Our task was to awaken the Wind$nCrystal, and so we have.
It has nothing to do with making$nanyone squirm.
Just watch, though. I bet he’ll$ntry to make everyone work just$nas hard, even with the wind$nback.
What? But that’s not…
Still, isn’t it better if we head$nhim off before he gets the$nchance?
Head him off how?
Step one is making it darned$nclear that it was [PCF1] that$nbrought the wind back.
And how do we do that?
Talk is cheap.$nWe show them proof!
…Ah!
The Wind Returns
Yes! The wind is back!
Just look at the sky!$nThe clouds are practically$nracing along!
Mmm, that breeze feels$nincredible!
Doubly so, after a job well$ndone!
All of Ancheim’s people were so$nthankful…
One girl said that thanks to the$nwind, her father is able to come$nhome again.
I heard, “We can all eat as a$nfamily again”!
Or, “Now we can fly kites!”$nYou should have seen the$nkids’ grins.
“All the skirts ruffling in the$nbreeze make me feel alive.”
Gee, I wonder who said THAT.
Ha ha ha! In any case,$nwe’ve got the wind at$nour back, now!
